seo.title: Appointment Booking Widget — Live-Calendar Embed for Any Site | AQ Marketing
seo.description: An embeddable appointment-booking widget that shows live calendar availability, takes deposits, and sends SMS + email confirmations. Wired to Jobber, Housecall Pro, Google Calendar, Acuity. Drops on any site.

sections.heading: Book it from your website. Not three voicemails later.
sections.lede: A booking widget on every page of your site. Live availability, your real calendar, optional deposit, automatic SMS + email confirmations and reminders. Cuts the phone-tag, no-shows, and "can you call me back" that lose jobs.

ctas.label: See it on a live site

sections.heading: What happens when you let people book themselves.
sections.intro: Self-serve booking removes the phone-tag and back-and-forth that loses jobs. Here's what the widget handles for home services, dental, and personal-care businesses.

stats.label: People can book outside business hours, when self-serve is on
SMS reminders plus an optional deposit keep more booked jobs on the calendar
Book in a few taps instead of rounds of phone-tag
No calendar clashes when the widget is the source of truth

sections.heading: Why phone-tag is the worst booking funnel.
sections.intro: Every back-and-forth is a chance for the buyer to drop. Most do. A quick "book now" experience is the difference between a job and a missed lead.

cards.title: Three rounds of phone-tag
cards.body: Customer calls, you're busy. You call back, they're at lunch. They call back, you're on a job. By round three, they've booked the next guy.

cards.title: Double-bookings + conflicts
cards.body: Two people on the team take bookings, calendars don't sync, you arrive at one job and the other one is screaming.

cards.title: No-shows
cards.body: Without an SMS reminder + a small deposit, no-show rates run 18–34% in MA service businesses. That's a half-day of crew time gone.

cards.title: "What time works for you" emails
cards.body: Endless email loops trying to find a slot. The customer abandons. You don't know they abandoned because they never said "no" — they just stopped replying.

sections.heading: What we wire into the booking flow.

items.title: Connects to your real tool
items.body: Jobber, Housecall Pro, ServiceTitan, Acuity, Calendly, Google Calendar, Square Appointments. Single source of truth — no double entry.

items.title: Deposit-on-book (optional)
items.body: Stripe-powered. $25–$100 holds the slot, applies to the invoice. Stops no-shows cold without scaring off legit customers.

items.title: Multi-channel reminders
items.body: Email confirmation immediately, SMS 24h before, second SMS 1h before. Reminders help cut down on no-shows.

items.title: Service-specific flows
items.body: Different forms per service. "Drain cleaning" needs photos; "consultation" doesn't. Smart branching, no irrelevant fields.

items.title: Service-area validation
items.body: Customer enters ZIP — if outside your service area, the form redirects to a wait-list capture instead of a booking. No wasted truck rolls.

items.title: Mobile-first
items.body: 60% of bookings happen on phones. Three taps from landing page to booked appointment, no pinch-zoom, no broken date pickers.

sections.heading: Live on your site in 7 business days.

steps.title: Pick the tool
steps.body: If you already have one (Jobber, etc.) we wire to it. If you don't, we recommend one and migrate any existing bookings.

steps.title: Build the flow
steps.body: Per-service forms, deposit rules, ZIP validation, reminder cadence. You approve every screen + every text/email template.

steps.title: Embed + brand
steps.body: Widget styled to your brand. Embedded site-wide, with a "Book now" CTA in the header, footer, and every service page.

steps.title: Go live + monitor
steps.body: First 30 bookings go through with us watching. Tune timing, copy, and friction points before handing off.

sections.heading: Why deposits are the single biggest no-show fix.
sections.prose_html:
Deposits work because they give the customer a stake. Charging a small refundable deposit ($25–$100) at booking meaningfully reduces no-shows. Customers who object to a deposit were often not going to show up anyway. Customers who pay one tend to keep the appointment.

The objection "customers will leave if I ask for a deposit" is real but smaller than owners think — a small share of bookings drop off when a deposit field is added. The rest who book are far more likely to actually show. Net: more booked-and-attended jobs, fewer crew hours wasted on empty driveways.

Pricing it

The booking widget is part of your Smart Website & Marketing Package at $500/mo; we confirm the exact scope at your free audit. Stripe processing fees on deposits are passed through (2.9% + $0.30) — you can absorb them or surcharge.

Where it fits with everything else

Pairs with the AI chatbot (which can book directly into the widget), the AI receptionist (which uses the same calendar), and SMS reminders. The full flow: visitor → chatbot/phone → real-time booking → SMS reminder → showed-up customer.

This is the standalone widget. The full engagement is different.

This page covers the Online Booking as a drop-in feature you can bolt onto any site. If you want the full done-for-you build — strategy, integration, ongoing optimization, and this widget as one piece of a unified system — that lives at the AI Websites agency engagement page. Different intent, different price point, different scope.

aside_items.note_body: A $50 deposit on a $400 service meaningfully cuts no-shows. The few who refuse to pay it weren't likely to show anyway. You net more booked-and-attended jobs.
60% of bookings happen on phones. Our widget is built mobile-first — three taps from landing to confirmation. Most competitor widgets break on iOS Safari.
WCAG 2.1 AA-tested. Keyboard navigation, screen reader support, color-contrast safe. Avoids the demand letters that hit small business sites every year.

items.q: Do I need a separate scheduling tool?
items.a: No — but it helps. We work with whatever you have (Jobber, Housecall Pro, Google Calendar, Acuity, etc.). If you don't have one, we'll recommend the right one for your industry as part of setup.

Yes. Stripe-powered, $25–$100 range, refundable per your policy. Deposits help cut down on no-shows. Optional per service — "consultations" can be no-deposit while "emergency dispatch" can require one.

Impossible if the widget is the source of truth. It checks live availability against your connected calendar before showing slots. Two people booking the same slot at the same instant is handled with optimistic locking.
Built mobile-first. 60% of bookings come from phones. Three taps from "book now" to confirmation, no pinch-zoom, no broken date pickers.

It's part of our Smart Website & Marketing Package at $500/mo (a 12-month agreement) — we confirm the exact scope at your free audit. Stripe processing fees (2.9% + $0.30) on deposits are passed through. No per-booking fees.
Yes — the widget reads from your real calendar. Block slots there, they disappear from the widget. Recurring blocks (every Wednesday, every other Friday) are supported.

cards.title: AI Chatbot
cards.body: Bot offers real calendar slots through the booking widget — "Wednesday at 2?" instead of "someone will reach out."

cards.title: SMS Reminders
cards.body: Automatic confirmations, day-before reminders, hour-before nudges. Helps cut down on no-shows.

cards.title: Review Automation
cards.body: After every completed booking, a 7-day-later review request. Booking → service → review, no manual chasing.

sections.eyebrow: Try it on a real site, not a demo
sections.heading: See booking on a live AQM client site — book a fake job in 90 seconds.
sections.body: We'll send you a sandbox link with a real booking flow on a real site. Click through, book a fake appointment, see the SMS confirmation hit your phone. Then decide.
